   176→// ── Gateway RPC helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   177→
   178→/// Call `device.token.create` on the gateway and print the token + command.
   179→async fn cmd_generate_token(host: &str, api_key: Option<&str>, name: Option<&str>) -> Result<()> {
   180→    let mut params = serde_json::Map::new();
   181→    if let Some(n) = name {
   182→        params.insert("displayName".into(), serde_json::json!(n));
   183→    }
   184→    params.insert("platform".into(), serde_json::json!("remote"));
   185→
   186→    let result = gateway_rpc(host, api_key, "device.token.create", params.into()).await?;
   187→
   188→    let token = result
   189→        .get("deviceToken")
   190→        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
   191→        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow::anyhow!("unexpected response: missing deviceToken"))?;
   192→
   193→    let ws_url = http_to_ws(host);
   194→
   195→    println!("Device token: {token}");
   196→    println!();
   197→    println!("Run this on the remote machine:");
   198→    println!("  moltis node add --host {ws_url} --token {token}");
   199→    println!();
   200→    println!("The token is shown once and cannot be retrieved later.");
   201→
   202→    Ok(())
   203→}
   204→
   205→/// Call `node.list` on the gateway and print connected nodes.
   206→async fn cmd_list(host: &str, api_key: Option<&str>) -> Result<()> {
   207→    let result = gateway_rpc(host, api_key, "node.list", serde_json::json!({})).await?;
   208→
   209→    let nodes = result
   210→        .get("nodes")
   211→        .and_then(|v| v.as_array())
   212→        .cloned()
   213→        .unwrap_or_default();
   214→
   215→    if nodes.is_empty() {
   216→        println!("No nodes connected.");
   217→        return Ok(());
   218→    }
   219→
   220→    for node in &nodes {
   221→        let id = node.get("nodeId").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or("?");
   222→        let name = node
   223→            .get("displayName")
   224→            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
   225→            .unwrap_or("(unnamed)");
   226→        let platform = node.get("platform").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or("?");
   227→
   228→        println!("{id}  {name}  ({platform})");
   229→    }
   230→
   231→    Ok(())
   232→}

   234→/// Send a single RPC request to the gateway over HTTP (JSON-RPC style).
   235→///
   236→/// The gateway exposes RPC methods over the WebSocket protocol, but for
   237→/// one-shot CLI commands we use the `/api/rpc` endpoint when available,
   238→/// falling back to a transient WebSocket connection.
   239→async fn gateway_rpc(
   240→    host: &str,
   241→    api_key: Option<&str>,
   242→    method: &str,
   243→    params: serde_json::Value,
   244→) -> Result<serde_json::Value> {
   245→    let url = format!("{}/api/rpc", host.trim_end_matches('/'));
   246→    let body = serde_json::json!({
   247→        "method": method,
   248→        "params": params,
   249→    });
   250→
   251→    let client = reqwest::Client::new();
   252→    let mut req = client.post(&url).json(&body);
   253→    if let Some(key) = api_key {
   254→        req = req.header("Authorization", format!("Bearer {key}"));
   255→    }
   256→
   257→    let resp = req
   258→        .send()
   259→        .await
   260→        .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("cannot reach gateway at {host}: {e}"))?;
   261→
   262→    if !resp.status().is_success() {
   263→        let status = resp.status();
   264→        let body = resp.text().await.unwrap_or_default();
   265→        anyhow::bail!("gateway returned {status}: {body}");
   266→    }
   267→
   268→    let result: serde_json::Value = resp.json().await?;
   269→
   270→    if let Some(err) = result.get("error") {
   271→        let msg = err
   272→            .get("message")
   273→            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
   274→            .unwrap_or("unknown error");
   275→        anyhow::bail!("RPC error: {msg}");
   276→    }
   277→
   278→    Ok(result.get("payload").cloned().unwrap_or(result))
   279→}
   280→
   281→/// Convert `http://host:port` to `ws://host:port/ws`.
   282→fn http_to_ws(http_url: &str) -> String {
   283→    let ws = http_url
   284→        .replace("https://", "wss://")
   285→        .replace("http://", "ws://");
   286→    let base = ws.trim_end_matches('/');
   287→    format!("{base}/ws")
   288→}

   544→/// Call `device.token.create` on the gateway and print the token + command.
   545→async fn cmd_generate_token(host: &str, api_key: Option<&str>, name: Option<&str>) -> Result<()> {
   546→    let mut params = serde_json::Map::new();
   547→    if let Some(n) = name {
   548→        params.insert("displayName".into(), serde_json::json!(n));
   549→    }
   550→    params.insert("platform".into(), serde_json::json!("remote"));
   551→
   552→    let result = gateway_rpc(host, api_key, "device.token.create", params.into()).await?;
   553→
   554→    let token = result
   555→        .get("deviceToken")
   556→        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
   557→        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow::anyhow!("unexpected response: missing deviceToken"))?;
   558→
   559→    let ws_url = http_to_ws(host);
   560→
   561→    println!("Device token: {token}");
   562→    println!();
   563→    println!("Run this on the remote machine:");
   564→    println!("  moltis node add --host {ws_url} --token {token}");
   565→    println!();
   566→    println!("The token is shown once and cannot be retrieved later.");
   567→
   568→    Ok(())
   569→}

   571→/// Call `node.list` on the gateway and print connected nodes.
   572→async fn cmd_list(host: &str, api_key: Option<&str>) -> Result<()> {
   573→    let result = gateway_rpc(host, api_key, "node.list", serde_json::json!({})).await?;
   574→
   575→    let nodes = result

   577→        .and_then(|v| v.as_array())
   578→        .cloned()
   579→        .unwrap_or_default();
   580→
   581→    if nodes.is_empty() {

   588→        let name = node

   600→/// Send a single RPC request to the gateway over HTTP (JSON-RPC style).
   601→///
   602→/// The gateway exposes RPC methods over the WebSocket protocol, but for
   603→/// one-shot CLI commands we use the `/api/rpc` endpoint when available,
   604→/// falling back to a transient WebSocket connection.
   605→async fn gateway_rpc(
   606→    host: &str,
   607→    api_key: Option<&str>,
   608→    method: &str,
   609→    params: serde_json::Value,
   610→) -> Result<serde_json::Value> {
   611→    let url = format!("{}/api/rpc", host.trim_end_matches('/'));
   612→    let body = serde_json::json!({
   613→        "method": method,
   614→        "params": params,
   615→    });
   616→
   617→    let client = reqwest::Client::new();
   618→    let mut req = client.post(&url).json(&body);
   619→    if let Some(key) = api_key {
   620→        req = req.header("Authorization", format!("Bearer {key}"));
   621→    }
   622→
   623→    let resp = req
   624→        .send()
   625→        .await
   626→        .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("cannot reach gateway at {host}: {e}"))?;
   627→
   628→    if !resp.status().is_success() {
   629→        let status = resp.status();
   630→        let body = resp.text().await.unwrap_or_default();
   631→        anyhow::bail!("gateway returned {status}: {body}");
   632→    }
   633→
   634→    let result: serde_json::Value = resp.json().await?;
   635→
   636→    if let Some(err) = result.get("error") {
   637→        let msg = err
   638→            .get("message")
   639→            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
   640→            .unwrap_or("unknown error");
   641→        anyhow::bail!("RPC error: {msg}");
   642→    }
   643→
   644→    Ok(result.get("payload").cloned().unwrap_or(result))
   645→}

   648→fn http_to_ws(http_url: &str) -> String {

   651→        .replace("http://", "ws://");

   106→/// Collect a point-in-time memory snapshot (process RSS + local llama.cpp +
   107→/// system memory).
   108→pub(crate) fn collect_mem_snapshot() -> MemSnapshot {
   109→    let mut sys = sysinfo::System::new();
   110→    sys.refresh_memory();
   111→    let pid = sysinfo::get_current_pid().ok();
   112→    if let Some(pid) = pid {
   113→        sys.refresh_processes_specifics(
   114→            sysinfo::ProcessesToUpdate::Some(&[pid]),
   115→            false,
   116→            sysinfo::ProcessRefreshKind::nothing().with_memory(),
   117→        );
   118→    }
   119→    let process = pid
   120→        .and_then(|p| sys.process(p))
   121→        .map(|p| p.memory())
   122→        .unwrap_or(0);
   123→    let local_llama_cpp = moltis_gateway::server::local_llama_cpp_bytes_for_ui();
   124→    let total = sys.total_memory();
   125→    // available_memory() returns 0 on macOS; fall back to total − used.
   126→    let available = match sys.available_memory() {
   127→        0 => total.saturating_sub(sys.used_memory()),
   128→        v => v,
   129→    };
   130→    MemSnapshot {
   131→        process,
   132→        local_llama_cpp: (local_llama_cpp > 0).then_some(local_llama_cpp),
   133→        available,
   134→        total,
   135→    }
   136→}

   208→            .map(|text| truncate_preview(text, SESSION_PREVIEW_MAX_CHARS));
   209→        let agent_id = entry.agent_id.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| "main".to_owned());
   210→        let agent_id_camel = agent_id.clone();
   211→
   212→        recent.push(serde_json::json!({
   213→            "id": entry.id,
   214→            "key": entry.key,
   215→            "label": entry.label,
   216→            "model": entry.model,
   217→            "createdAt": entry.created_at,
   218→            "updatedAt": entry.updated_at,
   219→            "messageCount": entry.message_count,
   220→            "lastSeenMessageCount": entry.last_seen_message_count,
   221→            "projectId": entry.project_id,
   222→            "sandbox_enabled": entry.sandbox_enabled,
   223→            "sandbox_image": entry.sandbox_image,
   224→            "worktree_branch": entry.worktree_branch,
   225→            "channelBinding": entry.channel_binding,
   226→            "activeChannel": active_channel,
   227→            "parentSessionKey": entry.parent_session_key,
   228→            "forkPoint": entry.fork_point,
   229→            "mcpDisabled": entry.mcp_disabled,
   230→            "preview": preview,
   231→            "archived": entry.archived,
   232→            "agent_id": agent_id,
   233→            "agentId": agent_id_camel,
   234→            "node_id": entry.node_id,
   235→            "version": entry.version,
   236→        }));
   237→    }
   238→
   239→    recent

   244→#[derive(Debug, Default, serde::Serialize)]
   245→pub(crate) struct NavCounts {
   246→    projects: usize,
   247→    providers: usize,
   248→    channels: usize,
   249→    skills: usize,
   250→    mcp: usize,
   251→    crons: usize,
   252→    hooks: usize,
   253→}
   254→
   255→pub(crate) async fn build_nav_counts(gw: &GatewayState) -> NavCounts {
   256→    let (projects, models, channels, mcp, crons) = tokio::join!(
   257→        gw.services.project.list(),
   258→        gw.services.model.list(),
   259→        gw.services.channel.status(),
   260→        gw.services.mcp.list(),
   261→        gw.services.cron.list(),
   262→    );
   263→
   264→    let projects = projects
   265→        .ok()
   266→        .and_then(|v| v.as_array().map(|a| a.len()))
   267→        .unwrap_or(0);
   268→
   269→    let providers = models
   270→        .ok()
   271→        .and_then(|v| {
   272→            v.as_array().map(|arr| {
   273→                let mut names: HashSet<&str> = HashSet::new();
   274→                for m in arr {
   275→                    if let Some(p) = m.get("provider").and_then(|p| p.as_str()) {
   276→                        names.insert(p);
   277→                    }
   278→                }
   279→                names.len()
   280→            })
   281→        })
   282→        .unwrap_or(0);
   283→
   284→    let channels = channels
   285→        .ok()
   286→        .and_then(|v| {
   287→            v.get("channels")
   288→                .and_then(|c| c.as_array())
   289→                .map(|a| a.len())
   290→        })
   291→        .unwrap_or(0);
   292→
   293→    let mut skills = 0usize;
   294→    if let Ok(path) = moltis_skills::manifest::ManifestStore::default_path() {
   295→        let store = moltis_skills::manifest::ManifestStore::new(path);
   296→        if let Ok(m) = store.load() {
   297→            skills = m
   298→                .repos
   299→                .iter()
   300→                .flat_map(|r| &r.skills)
   301→                .filter(|s| s.enabled)
   302→                .count();
   303→        }
   304→    }
   305→
   306→    let mcp = mcp
   307→        .ok()
   308→        .and_then(|v| {
   309→            v.as_array().map(|arr| {
   310→                arr.iter()
   311→                    .filter(|s| s.get("state").and_then(|s| s.as_str()) == Some("running"))
   312→                    .count()
   313→            })
   314→        })
   315→        .unwrap_or(0);
   316→
   317→    let crons = crons
   318→        .ok()
   319→        .and_then(|v| {
   320→            v.as_array().map(|arr| {
   321→                arr.iter()
   322→                    .filter(|j| {
   323→                        let enabled = j.get("enabled").and_then(|e| e.as_bool()).unwrap_or(false);
   324→                        let system = j.get("system").and_then(|s| s.as_bool()).unwrap_or(false);
   325→                        enabled && !system
   326→                    })
   327→                    .count()
   328→            })
   329→        })
   330→        .unwrap_or(0);
   331→
   332→    let hooks = gw.inner.read().await.discovered_hooks.len();
   333→
   334→    NavCounts {
   335→        projects,
   336→        providers,
   337→        channels,
   338→        skills,
   339→        mcp,
   340→        crons,
   341→        hooks,
   342→    }
   343→}

   347→pub(crate) async fn build_gon_data(gw: &GatewayState) -> GonData {
   348→    const GON_SESSIONS_RECENT_LIMIT: usize = 30;
   349→
   350→    let port = gw.port;
   351→    let identity = gw
   352→        .services
   353→        .onboarding
   354→        .identity_get()
   355→        .await
   356→        .ok()
   357→        .and_then(|v| serde_json::from_value(v).ok())
   358→        .unwrap_or_default();
   359→
   360→    let counts = build_nav_counts(gw).await;
   361→    let (crons, cron_status) = tokio::join!(gw.services.cron.list(), gw.services.cron.status());
   362→    let crons: Vec<moltis_cron::types::CronJob> = crons
   363→        .ok()
   364→        .and_then(|v| serde_json::from_value(v).ok())
   365→        .unwrap_or_default();
   366→    let cron_status: moltis_cron::types::CronStatus = cron_status
   367→        .ok()
   368→        .and_then(|v| serde_json::from_value(v).ok())
   369→        .unwrap_or_default();
   370→    let (heartbeat_config, channels_offered) = {
   371→        let inner = gw.inner.read().await;
   372→        (
   373→            inner.heartbeat_config.clone(),
   374→            inner.channels_offered.clone(),
   375→        )
   376→    };
   377→    let channel_descriptors: Vec<moltis_channels::ChannelDescriptor> = channels_offered
   378→        .iter()
   379→        .filter_map(|s| s.parse::<moltis_channels::ChannelType>().ok())
   380→        .map(|ct| ct.descriptor())
   381→        .collect();
   382→
   383→    let heartbeat_runs: Vec<moltis_cron::types::CronRunRecord> = gw
   384→        .services
   385→        .cron
   386→        .runs(serde_json::json!({ "id": "__heartbeat__", "limit": 10 }))
   387→        .await
   388→        .ok()
   389→        .and_then(|v| serde_json::from_value(v).ok())
   390→        .unwrap_or_default();
   391→
   392→    let sandbox = if let Some(ref router) = gw.sandbox_router {
   393→        SandboxGonInfo {
   394→            backend: router.backend_name().to_owned(),
   395→            os: std::env::consts::OS,
   396→            default_image: router.default_image().await,
   397→            image_building: router
   398→                .building_flag
   399→                .load(std::sync::atomic::Ordering::Relaxed),
   400→        }
   401→    } else {
   402→        SandboxGonInfo {
   403→            backend: "none".to_owned(),
   404→            os: std::env::consts::OS,
   405→            default_image: moltis_tools::sandbox::DEFAULT_SANDBOX_IMAGE.to_owned(),
   406→            image_building: false,
   407→        }
   408→    };
   409→
   410→    // Fetch agent personas for the gon data.
   411→    let agents: Vec<serde_json::Value> = if let Some(ref store) = gw.services.agent_persona_store {
   412→        store
   413→            .list()
   414→            .await
   415→            .ok()
   416→            .map(|list| {
   417→                list.into_iter()
   418→                    .map(|a| serde_json::to_value(a).unwrap_or_default())
   419→                    .collect()
   420→            })
   421→            .unwrap_or_default()
   422→    } else {
   423→        Vec::new()
   424→    };
   425→
   426→    let sessions_recent = build_recent_sessions_snapshot(gw, GON_SESSIONS_RECENT_LIMIT).await;
   427→
   428→    GonData {
   429→        identity,
   430→        version: gw.version.clone(),
   431→        port,
   432→        counts,
   433→        crons,
   434→        cron_status,
   435→        heartbeat_config,
   436→        heartbeat_runs,
   437→        voice_enabled: cfg!(feature = "voice"),
   438→        graphql_enabled: cfg!(feature = "graphql"),
   439→        git_branch: detect_git_branch(),
   440→        mem: collect_mem_snapshot(),
   441→        deploy_platform: gw.deploy_platform.clone(),
   442→        channels_offered,
   443→        channel_descriptors,
   444→        update: gw.inner.read().await.update.clone(),
   445→        sandbox,
   446→        routes: SPA_ROUTES.clone(),
   447→        started_at: *PROCESS_STARTED_AT_MS,
   448→        openclaw_detected: moltis_gateway::server::openclaw_detected_for_ui(),
   449→        sessions_recent,
   450→        agents,
   451→        #[cfg(feature = "vault")]
   452→        vault_status: {
   453→            if let Some(ref vault) = gw.vault {
   454→                match vault.status().await {
   455→                    Ok(s) => format!("{s:?}").to_lowercase(),
   456→                    Err(_) => "error".to_owned(),
   457→                }
   458→            } else {
   459→                "disabled".to_owned()
   460→            }
   461→        },
   462→    }
   463→}

   547→pub(crate) fn script_safe_json<T: serde::Serialize>(value: &T) -> String {
   548→    let json = match serde_json::to_string(value) {
   549→        Ok(json) => json,
   550→        Err(e) => {
   551→            warn!(error = %e, "failed to serialize gon data for html template");
   552→            "{}".to_owned()
   553→        },
   554→    };
   555→    json.replace('<', "\\u003c")
   556→        .replace('>', "\\u003e")
   557→        .replace('&', "\\u0026")
   558→        .replace('\u{2028}', "\\u2028")
   559→        .replace('\u{2029}', "\\u2029")
   560→}

   567→        .map(str::trim)
   568→        .filter(|name| !name.is_empty());
   569→
   570→    let title = match user_name {
   571→        Some(user_name) => format!("{agent_name}: {user_name} AI assistant"),
   572→        None => format!("{agent_name}: AI assistant"),
   573→    };
   574→    let description = match user_name {
   575→        Some(user_name) => format!(
   576→            "{agent_name} is {user_name}'s personal AI assistant. Multi-provider models, tools, memory, sandboxed execution, and channel access in one Rust binary."
   577→        ),
   578→        None => format!(
   579→            "{agent_name} is a personal AI assistant. Multi-provider models, tools, memory, sandboxed execution, and channel access in one Rust binary."
   580→        ),
   581→    };
   582→    let image_alt = format!("{agent_name} - personal AI assistant");
   583→
   584→    ShareMeta {
   585→        title,
   586→        description,
   587→        site_name: agent_name.to_owned(),

   601→pub(crate) async fn render_spa_template(
   602→    gateway: &GatewayState,
   603→    template: SpaTemplate,
   604→) -> axum::response::Response {
   605→    let (build_ts, asset_prefix) = if is_dev_assets() {
   606→        let ts = std::time::SystemTime::now()
   607→            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)
   608→            .unwrap_or_default()
   609→            .as_millis();
   610→        ("dev".to_owned(), format!("/assets/v/{ts}/"))
   611→    } else {
   612→        static HASH: std::sync::LazyLock<String> = std::sync::LazyLock::new(asset_content_hash);
   613→        (HASH.to_string(), format!("/assets/v/{}/", *HASH))
   614→    };
   615→
   616→    let nonce = uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string();
   617→
   618→    // Resolve Shiki URL from config override or default CDN.
   619→    let shiki_url = gateway
   620→        .inner
   621→        .read()
   622→        .await
   623→        .shiki_cdn_url
   624→        .clone()
   625→        .unwrap_or_else(|| DEFAULT_SHIKI_CDN_URL.to_owned());
   626→
   627→    let body = match template {
   628→        SpaTemplate::Index => {
   629→            let gon = build_gon_data(gateway).await;
   630→            let share_meta = build_share_meta(&gon.identity);
   631→            let gon_json = script_safe_json(&gon);
   632→            let template = IndexHtmlTemplate {
   633→                build_ts: &build_ts,
   634→                asset_prefix: &asset_prefix,
   635→                nonce: &nonce,
   636→                gon_json: &gon_json,
   637→                share_title: &share_meta.title,
   638→                share_description: &share_meta.description,
   639→                share_site_name: &share_meta.site_name,
   640→                share_image_url: SHARE_IMAGE_URL,
   641→                share_image_alt: &share_meta.image_alt,
   642→                routes: &SPA_ROUTES,
   643→                shiki_url: &shiki_url,
   644→            };
   645→            match template.render() {
   646→                Ok(html) => html,
   647→                Err(e) => {
   648→                    warn!(error = %e, "failed to render index template");
   649→                    String::new()
   650→                },
   651→            }
   652→        },
   653→        SpaTemplate::Login => {
   654→            let gon = build_gon_data(gateway).await;
   655→            let gon_json = script_safe_json(&gon);
   656→            let page_title = identity_name(&gon.identity).to_owned();
   657→            let template = LoginHtmlTemplate {
   658→                build_ts: &build_ts,
   659→                asset_prefix: &asset_prefix,
   660→                nonce: &nonce,
   661→                page_title: &page_title,
   662→                gon_json: &gon_json,
   663→            };
   664→            match template.render() {
   665→                Ok(html) => html,
   666→                Err(e) => {
   667→                    warn!(error = %e, "failed to render login template");
   668→                    String::new()
   669→                },
   670→            }
   671→        },
   672→        SpaTemplate::Onboarding => {
   673→            let gon = build_gon_data(gateway).await;
   674→            let gon_json = script_safe_json(&gon);
   675→            let page_title = format!("{} onboarding", identity_name(&gon.identity));
   676→            let template = OnboardingHtmlTemplate {
   677→                build_ts: &build_ts,
   678→                asset_prefix: &asset_prefix,
   679→                nonce: &nonce,
   680→                page_title: &page_title,
   681→                gon_json: &gon_json,
   682→            };
   683→            match template.render() {
   684→                Ok(html) => html,
   685→                Err(e) => {
   686→                    warn!(error = %e, "failed to render onboarding template");
   687→                    String::new()
   688→                },
   689→            }
   690→        },
   691→        SpaTemplate::SetupRequired => {
   692→            let template = SetupRequiredHtmlTemplate {
   693→                asset_prefix: &asset_prefix,
   694→            };
   695→            match template.render() {
   696→                Ok(html) => html,
   697→                Err(e) => {
   698→                    warn!(error = %e, "failed to render setup-required template");
   699→                    String::new()
   700→                },
   701→            }
   702→        },
   703→    };
   704→
   705→    // Extract CDN origin from shiki_url for CSP script-src allowlisting.
   706→    let shiki_csp_origin = url::Url::parse(&shiki_url)
   707→        .ok()
   708→        .and_then(|u| u.host_str().map(|host| format!("{}://{host}", u.scheme())));
   709→
   710→    let csp = format!(
   711→        "default-src 'self'; \
   712→         script-src 'self' 'nonce-{nonce}' 'wasm-unsafe-eval'{shiki_origin}; \
   713→         style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; \
   714→         img-src 'self' data: blob:; \
   715→         media-src 'self' blob:; \
   716→         font-src 'self'; \
   717→         connect-src 'self' ws: wss:; \
   718→         frame-ancestors 'none'; \
   719→         form-action 'self'; \
   720→         base-uri 'self'; \
   721→         object-src 'none'",
   722→        shiki_origin = shiki_csp_origin
   723→            .as_deref()
   724→            .map(|o| format!(" {o}"))
   725→            .unwrap_or_default(),
   726→    );
   727→
   728→    let mut response = Html(body).into_response();
   729→    let headers = response.headers_mut();
   730→    if let Ok(val) = "no-cache, no-store".parse() {
   731→        headers.insert(axum::http::header::CACHE_CONTROL, val);
   732→    }
   733→    if let Ok(val) = csp.parse() {
   734→        headers.insert(axum::http::header::CONTENT_SECURITY_POLICY, val);
   735→    }
   736→    response
   737→}

// ── Shared app state ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

#[derive(Clone)]
pub struct AppState {
    pub gateway: Arc<GatewayState>,
    pub methods: Arc<MethodRegistry>,
    pub request_throttle: Arc<crate::request_throttle::RequestThrottle>,
    pub webauthn_registry: Option<SharedWebAuthnRegistry>,
    #[cfg(feature = "push-notifications")]
    pub push_service: Option<Arc<crate::push::PushService>>,
    #[cfg(feature = "graphql")]
    pub graphql_schema: moltis_graphql::MoltisSchema,
}

/// Function signature for adding extra routes (e.g. web-UI) to the gateway.
pub type RouteEnhancer = fn() -> Router<AppState>;

// ── Server startup ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

/// Build the API routes (shared between both build_gateway_app versions).
///
/// Auth is enforced by `auth_gate` middleware on the whole router — these
/// routes no longer carry their own auth layer.
/// Add feature-specific routes to API routes.
/// Build the CORS layer with dynamic host-based origin validation.
///
/// Instead of `allow_origin(Any)`, this validates the `Origin` header against the
/// request's `Host` header using the same `is_same_origin` logic as the WebSocket
/// CSWSH protection. This is secure for Docker/cloud deployments where the hostname
/// is unknown at build time — the server dynamically allows its own origin at
/// request time.
fn build_cors_layer() -> CorsLayer {
    CorsLayer::new()
        .allow_origin(AllowOrigin::predicate(
            |origin: &axum::http::HeaderValue, parts: &axum::http::request::Parts| {
                let origin_str = origin.to_str().unwrap_or("");
                let host = parts
                    .headers
                    .get(axum::http::header::HOST)
                    .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())
                    .unwrap_or("");
                is_same_origin(origin_str, host)
            },
        ))
        .allow_methods(Any)
        .allow_headers(Any)
}

/// 2 MiB global request body limit — sufficient for any JSON API payload, small
/// enough to limit abuse. The upload endpoint has its own 25 MiB limit.
const REQUEST_BODY_LIMIT: usize = 2 * 1024 * 1024;

/// Apply the full middleware stack to the router.
///
/// Layer order (outermost → innermost for requests):
/// 1. `CatchPanicLayer` — converts handler panics to 500s
/// 2. `SetSensitiveHeadersLayer` — marks Authorization/Cookie as redacted
/// 3. `SetRequestIdLayer` — generates x-request-id before tracing
/// 4. `TraceLayer` (optional) — logs requests with redacted headers + request ID
/// 5. `CorsLayer` — handles preflight; logged by trace
/// 6. `PropagateRequestIdLayer` — copies x-request-id to response
/// 7. Security response headers — X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, etc.
/// 8. `RequestBodyLimitLayer` — rejects oversized bodies
/// 9. `CompressionLayer` (innermost) — compresses response body
fn apply_middleware_stack<S>(
    router: Router<S>,
    cors: CorsLayer,
    http_request_logs: bool,
) -> Router<S>
where
    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,
{
    use axum::http::{HeaderValue, header};

    // Inner layers: compression, body limit, security headers, request ID propagation.
    let router = router
        .layer(CompressionLayer::new())
        .layer(tower_http::limit::RequestBodyLimitLayer::new(
            REQUEST_BODY_LIMIT,
        ))
        .layer(SetResponseHeaderLayer::overriding(
            header::HeaderName::from_static("x-content-type-options"),
            HeaderValue::from_static("nosniff"),
        ))
        .layer(SetResponseHeaderLayer::overriding(
            header::HeaderName::from_static("x-frame-options"),
            HeaderValue::from_static("sameorigin"),
        ))
        .layer(SetResponseHeaderLayer::overriding(
            header::HeaderName::from_static("referrer-policy"),
            HeaderValue::from_static("strict-origin-when-cross-origin"),
        ))
        .layer(PropagateRequestIdLayer::x_request_id())
        .layer(cors);

    // Optional trace layer — sees redacted headers and request ID.
    let router = apply_http_trace_layer(router, http_request_logs);

    // Outer layers: request ID generation, sensitive header marking, panic catching.
    router
        .layer(SetRequestIdLayer::x_request_id(MakeRequestUuid))
        .layer(SetSensitiveHeadersLayer::new([
            header::AUTHORIZATION,
            header::COOKIE,
            header::SET_COOKIE,
        ]))
        .layer(CatchPanicLayer::new())
}

/// Apply optional HTTP request/response tracing layer.
fn apply_http_trace_layer<S>(router: Router<S>, enabled: bool) -> Router<S>
where
    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,
{
    if enabled {
        let http_trace = TraceLayer::new_for_http()
            .make_span_with(|request: &axum::http::Request<_>| {
                let request_id = request
                    .headers()
                    .get("x-request-id")
                    .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())
                    .unwrap_or("-")
                    .to_owned();
                let user_agent = request
                    .headers()
                    .get(axum::http::header::USER_AGENT)
                    .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())
                    .unwrap_or("-")
                    .to_owned();
                let referer = request
                    .headers()
                    .get(axum::http::header::REFERER)
                    .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())
                    .unwrap_or("-")
                    .to_owned();
                tracing::info_span!(
                    "http_request",
                    method = %request.method(),
                    uri = %request.uri(),
                    request_id = %request_id,
                    user_agent = %user_agent,
                    referer = %referer
                )
            })
            .on_request(DefaultOnRequest::new().level(Level::INFO))
            .on_response(DefaultOnResponse::new().level(Level::INFO));
        router.layer(http_trace)
    } else {
        router
    }
}

/// Build the gateway base router and `AppState` without throttle, middleware,
/// or state consumption. Callers can merge additional routes (e.g. web-UI)
/// before calling [`finalize_gateway_app`].
#[cfg(feature = "push-notifications")]
pub fn build_gateway_base(
    state: Arc<GatewayState>,
    methods: Arc<MethodRegistry>,
    push_service: Option<Arc<crate::push::PushService>>,
    webauthn_registry: Option<SharedWebAuthnRegistry>,
) -> (Router<AppState>, AppState) {
    let mut router = Router::new()
        .route("/health", get(health_handler))
        .route("/ws/chat", get(ws_upgrade_handler));

    // Nest auth routes if credential store is available.
    if let Some(ref cred_store) = state.credential_store {
        let auth_state = AuthState {
            credential_store: Arc::clone(cred_store),
            webauthn_registry: webauthn_registry.clone(),
            gateway_state: Arc::clone(&state),
        };
        router = router.nest("/api/auth", auth_router().with_state(auth_state));
    }

    #[cfg(feature = "graphql")]
    let graphql_schema = {
        let system_info = Arc::new(crate::graphql_routes::GatewaySystemInfoService {
            state: Arc::clone(&state),
        });
        let services = state.services.to_services(system_info);
        moltis_graphql::build_schema(services, state.graphql_broadcast.clone())
    };

    let app_state = AppState {
        gateway: state,
        methods,
        request_throttle: Arc::new(crate::request_throttle::RequestThrottle::new()),
        webauthn_registry: webauthn_registry.clone(),
        push_service,
        #[cfg(feature = "graphql")]
        graphql_schema,
    };

    // GraphQL routes — auth is handled by the global auth_gate in
    // finalize_gateway_app.
    #[cfg(feature = "graphql")]
    {
        router = router.route(
            "/graphql",
            get(crate::graphql_routes::graphql_get_handler)
                .post(crate::graphql_routes::graphql_handler),
        );
    }

    (router, app_state)
}

/// Build the gateway base router and `AppState` without throttle, middleware,
/// or state consumption. Callers can merge additional routes (e.g. web-UI)
/// before calling [`finalize_gateway_app`].
#[cfg(not(feature = "push-notifications"))]
pub fn build_gateway_base(
    state: Arc<GatewayState>,
    methods: Arc<MethodRegistry>,
    webauthn_registry: Option<SharedWebAuthnRegistry>,
) -> (Router<AppState>, AppState) {
    let mut router = Router::new()
        .route("/health", get(health_handler))
        .route("/ws/chat", get(ws_upgrade_handler));

    // Add Prometheus metrics endpoint (unauthenticated for scraping).
    #[cfg(feature = "prometheus")]
    {
        router = router.route(
            "/metrics",
            get(crate::metrics_routes::prometheus_metrics_handler),
        );
    }

    // Nest auth routes if credential store is available.
    if let Some(ref cred_store) = state.credential_store {
        let auth_state = AuthState {
            credential_store: Arc::clone(cred_store),
            webauthn_registry: webauthn_registry.clone(),
            gateway_state: Arc::clone(&state),
        };
        router = router.nest("/api/auth", auth_router().with_state(auth_state));
    }

    #[cfg(feature = "graphql")]
    let graphql_schema = {
        let system_info = Arc::new(crate::graphql_routes::GatewaySystemInfoService {
            state: Arc::clone(&state),
        });
        let services = state.services.to_services(system_info);
        moltis_graphql::build_schema(services, state.graphql_broadcast.clone())
    };

    let app_state = AppState {
        gateway: state,
        methods,
        request_throttle: Arc::new(crate::request_throttle::RequestThrottle::new()),
        webauthn_registry: webauthn_registry.clone(),
        #[cfg(feature = "graphql")]
        graphql_schema,
    };

    // GraphQL routes — auth is handled by the global auth_gate in
    // finalize_gateway_app.
    #[cfg(feature = "graphql")]
    {
        router = router.route(
            "/graphql",
            get(crate::graphql_routes::graphql_get_handler)
                .post(crate::graphql_routes::graphql_handler),
        );
    }

    (router, app_state)
}

/// Apply throttle, auth gate, middleware, and state to a base router,
/// producing the final `Router` ready for `axum::serve`.
pub fn finalize_gateway_app(
    router: Router<AppState>,
    app_state: AppState,
    http_request_logs: bool,
) -> Router {
    let cors = build_cors_layer();
    // Auth gate covers the entire router — public paths are exempted inside
    // `is_public_path()`.  Only compiled when the web-ui feature is enabled
    // (matches the old architecture where auth_gate was global).
    #[cfg(feature = "web-ui")]
    let router = router.layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(
        app_state.clone(),
        crate::auth_middleware::auth_gate,
    ));
    // Vault guard blocks API requests when the vault is sealed (not
    // uninitialized). Applied after auth_gate so sealed state is checked
    // only for authenticated requests.
    #[cfg(feature = "vault")]
    let router = router.layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(
        app_state.clone(),
        crate::auth_middleware::vault_guard,
    ));
    let router = router.layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(
        app_state.clone(),
        crate::request_throttle::throttle_gate,
    ));
    let router = apply_middleware_stack(router, cors, http_request_logs);
    router.with_state(app_state)
}

/// Convenience wrapper: build base + finalize in one call (used by tests).
#[cfg(feature = "push-notifications")]
pub fn build_gateway_app(
    state: Arc<GatewayState>,
    methods: Arc<MethodRegistry>,
    push_service: Option<Arc<crate::push::PushService>>,
    http_request_logs: bool,
    webauthn_registry: Option<SharedWebAuthnRegistry>,
) -> Router {
    let (router, app_state) = build_gateway_base(state, methods, push_service, webauthn_registry);
    finalize_gateway_app(router, app_state, http_request_logs)
}

/// Convenience wrapper: build base + finalize in one call (used by tests).
#[cfg(not(feature = "push-notifications"))]
pub fn build_gateway_app(
    state: Arc<GatewayState>,
    methods: Arc<MethodRegistry>,
    http_request_logs: bool,
    webauthn_registry: Option<SharedWebAuthnRegistry>,
) -> Router {
    let (router, app_state) = build_gateway_base(state, methods, webauthn_registry);
    finalize_gateway_app(router, app_state, http_request_logs)
}

async fn maybe_deliver_cron_output(
    outbound: Option<Arc<dyn moltis_channels::ChannelOutbound>>,
    req: &moltis_cron::service::AgentTurnRequest,
    delivery_text: &str,
) {
    if !req.deliver || delivery_text.trim().is_empty() {
        return;
    }

    let (Some(channel_account), Some(chat_id)) = (&req.channel, &req.to) else {
        return;
    };

    if let Some(outbound) = outbound {
        if let Err(error) = outbound
            .send_text(channel_account, chat_id, delivery_text, None)
            .await
        {
            tracing::warn!(
                channel = %channel_account,
                to = %chat_id,
                error = %error,
                "cron job channel delivery failed"
            );
        }
    } else {
        tracing::debug!("cron job delivery requested but no channel outbound configured");
    }
}

i = 0
while i < len(lines):
    line = lines[i]
    if line.startswith('<<<<<<< HEAD'):
        # Find the sections
        head_start = i + 1
        base_start = None
        separator = None
        end = None
        j = i + 1
        depth = 0
        while j < len(lines):
            if lines[j].startswith('<<<<<<< '):
                depth += 1
            elif lines[j].startswith('||||||| ') and depth == 0:
                base_start = j + 1
            elif lines[j].startswith('=======\n') and depth == 0:
                separator = j
            elif lines[j].startswith('>>>>>>> ') and depth == 0:
                end = j
                break
            elif lines[j].startswith('>>>>>>> '):
                depth -= 1
            j += 1
        
        if end is None:
            # Orphan marker, just skip it
            result.append(line)
            i += 1
            continue
        
        head_end = base_start - 1 if base_start else (separator if separator else end)
        head_lines = lines[head_start:head_end]
        
        if separator is not None:
            theirs_lines = lines[separator + 1:end]
        else:
            theirs_lines = []
        
        # Keep HEAD (httpd-crate branch) for all remaining conflicts
        # HEAD has the transport-extracted version
        for hl in head_lines:
            result.append(hl)
        
        i = end + 1
    else:
        result.append(line)
        i += 1

with open('crates/gateway/src/server.rs', 'w') as f:
    f.writelines(result)

print("Done - resolved all remaining conflicts keeping HEAD")
